requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want discover skill to load only once and arguments not duplicated, so that ~39K context is not wasted on duplicate injection and argument copying

acceptance_criteria.ears: IF discover skill has already been injected into context, THEN Skill tool invocation SHALL return 'already loaded' without re-injecting content
WHEN /discover command is invoked with arguments, THEN arguments SHALL appear only in the user message block, NOT appended to skill text as an ARGUMENTS section

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Modify SKILL.md template to remove ARGUMENTS append logic; add dedup guard for Skill tool re-invocation

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want codebase exploration to run in a subagent, so that main agent only receives a mode verdict (~500 chars) instead of 23K of file reads

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN mode detection begins, THEN a subagent SHALL be dispatched with contract { input: project root path, output_summary: 'mode=greenfield|feature + one-line rationale' }
IF subagent returns, THEN main agent context increase SHALL be < 1K chars

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Rewrite mode-detection.md to use dispatch contract instead of inline Read/Glob

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want competitive research and direction generation to run in a subagent, so that main agent only receives structured direction summaries (~2K) instead of 18K

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN Layer 1 direction generation begins, THEN a subagent SHALL be dispatched to perform competitive research and generate 3-5 directions
IF subagent returns, THEN summary in main context SHALL contain direction name + differentiator + biggest risk per direction, total < 3K chars

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Add dispatch contract to SKILL.md Layer 1 section

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want requirement lock table generation to run in a subagent reading the full artifact, so that main agent only receives a summary (~2K) instead of 24K

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN user approves Layer 2, THEN Layer 3 generation SHALL be dispatched to a subagent that reads all files under specs/discover/ (or specs/features/feat-{featureSlug}/discover/) directory
IF subagent returns, THEN main agent SHALL receive requirement count + invariant count + high-risk items list, total < 3K chars
IF user requests REVISE on specific requirements, THEN revision SHALL be dispatched to subagent with revision instructions

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Restructure SKILL.md Layer 3 to dispatch-first; main agent only presents summary and collects user actions

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want artifact JSON construction and file writing to run in a subagent, so that main agent only receives a completion confirmation (~500 chars) instead of 22K

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN Layer 3 is approved, THEN artifact writing SHALL be dispatched to a subagent
IF subagent returns, THEN main agent SHALL receive 'artifact written to [path]' confirmation, < 500 chars

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Wrap artifact-writer.md as subagent dispatch target

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want all Stitch MCP calls to run in a subagent, returning only screen ID + description summary (~1K per screen), so that 353K of raw component JSON never enters main context

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN UI Taste exploration calls Stitch MCP, THEN all Stitch calls SHALL execute in a subagent
IF subagent returns screen results, THEN summary SHALL contain screen ID + one-line visual description per screen, total < 2K per batch
IF user requests iteration on specific screens, THEN iteration dispatch SHALL include user feedback + target screen IDs

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Rewrite ui-taste.md to use dispatch contract for all Stitch interactions

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want each subagent dispatch to follow a standardized contract, so that behavior is consistent across stages and platforms

acceptance_criteria.ears: EVERY dispatch contract SHALL contain: task (string), input (file paths), output_file (path), output_summary (format + max size)
EVERY dispatch contract SHALL be written as HTML comment block <!-- DISPATCH CONTRACT ... --> in the skill markdown

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Define dispatch contract schema; embed in all affected sub-skills

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want the system to stop and ask me 'retry or fallback?' when a subagent dispatch fails, so that I maintain control over error recovery

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN subagent dispatch fails (timeout/error/unsupported), THEN main agent SHALL stop and present error to user
WHEN error is presented, THEN main agent SHALL ask 'retry or fallback to main agent execution?'
IF user chooses fallback, THEN main agent SHALL execute the stage inline with a warning about increased context consumption
WHEN subagent dispatch fails after partial output_file write, THEN the partial file SHALL be deleted before retry or fallback

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Add error handling template to dispatch contract standard; include partial file cleanup step

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want dispatch contracts to be platform-agnostic, so that Claude Code / Codex / OpenCode all execute the same discover skill correctly

acceptance_criteria.ears: Dispatch contracts SHALL use platform-agnostic language (no Claude Code-specific tool names)
Each platform's AI agent SHALL interpret dispatch contracts and invoke subagents using that platform's native mechanism (Claude Code: Agent tool, Codex CLI: subagent spawn, OpenCode: Task tool)

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Review all dispatch contracts for platform-specific language; document mapping for Codex TOML and OpenCode YAML

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want the total main-agent context to stay under 200K chars for a complete discover run, so that the skill works on 256K context models

acceptance_criteria.ears: IF a complete discover run (greenfield, full mode, 3 rounds UI iteration) completes, THEN cumulative main-agent user+assistant message content (excluding system prompts and tool schemas) SHALL be < 200K chars
THE SYSTEM SHALL continue to produce identical discover.json artifact structure when context-diet optimizations are applied

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Verification via simulated discover run with context measurement

invariants.statement: discover.json output schema SHALL remain unchanged regardless of subagent usage
User-visible interaction flow (questions, choices, confirmations) SHALL remain functionally identical
All user decisions SHALL be persisted to artifact files before any subagent dispatch that depends on them
